scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute this suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued in law. The Defendant resides at Koramangala, Bengaluru, and the grounds for this suit arose within Bengaluru where the Plaintiff resides at Jayanagar, Bengaluru. Thus, this Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The value of the suit is within the pecuniary limits of this Court.

LIMITATION

2. The present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under the applicable article of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963. The cause of action is subsisting and has not been barred by law.

FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION

3. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant as a hand loan. The amount was transferred via NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR Number AXIB20240315123456, as evidenced by the bank record marked as Annexure A. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan and the liability to repay through WhatsApp messages, marked as Annexure B. The loan was advanced on the understanding that it would be repaid on demand.

DEFAULT AND DEMAND

4. The repayment became due on 20.06.2024, which is the date of default agreed upon for the purpose of interest calculation. Despite the liability becoming due, the Defendant failed to repay the amount. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands and subsequently issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, marked as Annexure C. The Defendant has failed to respond to the said notice and has refused to repay the outstanding amount.

QUANTIFICATION OF CLAIM

5. The principal amount due from the Defendant is Rs. 15,00,000. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation. The interest is claimed on a simple interest basis as there is no express agreement for compound interest. The total claim comprises the principal amount along with the accrued interest calculated upto the date of filing of this plaint.

LEGAL BASIS

6. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the money lent under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant has committed a breach of the obligation to repay the loan. The civil court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The territorial jurisdiction is governed by Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the Defendant resides and the grounds for suit arose within Bengaluru. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The loan was advanced for lawful consideration, proved by the bank transfer trail, and the Defendant's acknowledgment via digital communication precludes any defence regarding lack of consideration or limitation. The rate of interest claimed is within lawful limits for unsecured lending.

CAUSE OF ACTION

7. The grounds for suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant failed to repay the loan on the date of default. Further, the grounds for suit arose on 01.07.2024 when the legal notice was issued and the Defendant failed to comply with the demand for repayment. The suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed by law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

8. For the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee, the suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000 being the principal amount, plus the accrued interest. The requisite court fee has been paid on the plaint under the applicable Court Fee Act. The amount of court fee paid is {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}. The plaint is properly stamped and tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

9. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

10. In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
(a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the recovery of Rs. 15,00,000 towards the principal loan amount;
(b) Award interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal amount from 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation;
(c) Award fut
